title: Iran Blocks Oil Ships in Important Waterway
summary: Iran has attacked ships in the Strait of Hormuz, a narrow waterway between Iran and the Arabian Peninsula that is very important for oil shipping. This happened after the United States and Israel attacked Iranian targets. Iran says it will stop oil from passing through the strait to the U.S. and its allies. The attacks have made many countries worried because they need oil from this area. Countries are trying to keep oil flowing, but the situation is tense. This story shows how conflicts can affect global energy supplies and cause fear around the world.

a type of fuel that many countries need for energy
a country in the Middle East that is attacking ships
a country that attacked Iran and is affected by the oil blockage
a country that attacked Iran with the U.S.
large boats that carry goods like oil across the water

background_read: The Strait of Hormuz is a very important waterway in the Persian Gulf. It is like a narrow door that many oil ships must pass through to deliver oil from countries like Saudi Arabia to the rest of the world. Oil is a key source of energy for cars, factories, and homes. When there is trouble in this area, it can make oil harder to get and more expensive, which affects everyone. Iran is a country with a lot of power in this region, and conflicts with other countries like the U.S. and Israel can lead to big problems for global trade.
Article_Structure: WHO: Iran, the United States, Israel, and other countries
WHAT: Iran attacked ships in the Strait of Hormuz to block oil after being attacked by the U.S. and Israel
WHERE: Strait of Hormuz, a narrow waterway between Iran and the Arabian Peninsula
WHY: To retaliate against attacks and stop oil from reaching enemies, causing global energy fears

perspectives.description: Iran sees the attacks as a response to being attacked by the U.S. and Israel, and it wants to protect its interests by blocking oil to its enemies.
